Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on CentOS 8.3/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest from trevis-209vm13: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/5b365896-f839-43f6-b0a8-2bfd591a7349 ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

(1 comment)

The patch itself is fine, but to be honest I don't think what is left in this header looks very useful.  The lustre_posix_acl_*() functions in obdclass/acl.c are only ever used in mdd/mdd_permission.c, so that whole file could be moved over to lustre/mdd/acl.c and the few remaining function prototypes in lustre_eacl.h moved into mdd/mdd_internal.h.

Maybe in a follow-on patch, since this one is ready to go.

It looks like the last user of this code in the client was removed in Lustre commit v2_8_54_0-73-g9d06de3973 "LU-6971 cleanup: not support remote client anymore".
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

> The patch itself is fine, but to be honest I don't think what is left in this header looks very useful. 

excellent idea - thanks.

parents.subject: LU-13783 libcfs: don't depend on sysctl support for debugfs

commit.subject: LU-14291 lustre: clean up lustre_eacl.h and make server-only
commit.message:
LU-14291 lustre: clean up lustre_eacl.h and make server-only

lustre_eacl.h contains a number of declarations that are never used:
remove them.

The declarations which are used are only needed on server-side files,
so remove the #include from elsewhere.

As obdclass/acl.c is only built server-side, remove the
 #ifdef HAVE_SERVER_SUPPORT
in the file.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: If1a3d908bf8357041c38ab9d335efa1e051cef16
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/41126
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
